findings.title: happy_path_receipt carries 3 fields not enumerated in happy_path_receipt_fields
findings.description: The emitted happy_path_receipt object contains 15 keys, but the declared receipt field manifest (policy.requiredHappyPathReceiptFields, mirrored to result.happy_path_receipt_fields and the fixture) enumerates only 12, omitting matter_id, audit_hint_policy_ref, and decision_reason. assertControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetPrimaryHappyPathResult does independently validate all 15 receipt keys, so there is no validation gap or boundary risk; however a downstream consumer (e.g. RP00.P02.M07.S08) iterating happy_path_receipt_fields to enumerate the receipt would not discover those 3 fields.
findings.recommendation: Non-blocking. Either keep the current required-subset semantics (the field is named 'required...'), documenting that happy_path_receipt may carry additional metadata keys, or expose a separate full-receipt field manifest so downstream enumeration is complete. Safe to defer.

parsed_review.summary: RP00.P02.M07.S07 is a correctly-scoped, metadata-only Risk-A boundary unit. precheckControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetPrimaryHappyPath consumes only the S06 audit-hint precheck result (validates entrypoint=test_and_golden_case_set, audit_hint_precheck_subphase_id=RP00.P02.M07.S06, audit_hint present, nonblank audit_event_ref, audit_hint_decision=allow_metadata_only, audit_event_ref_satisfied=true via assertControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetAuditHintPrecheckResult), then returns a deeply Object.frozen result plus a frozen happy_path_receipt. The function performs NO I/O of any kind: no test/golden generation or persistence, no audit ledger append or audit event write, no permission-engine/AuthZ/security-trimming evaluation, no runtime route/service execution, and no DB/storage/product-state writes (every corresponding *_permitted flag is hard-coded false and re-asserted by the validator). It fails closed on any happyPathClaims value that is not undefined/null/false — rejecting both the 29 enumerated forbidden claims and any unknown claim, plus non-object claim inputs. All data is synthetic (tenant.synthetic.lawfirm, actor user.synthetic.control-plane-admin, matter_id strictly null, golden/unit/permission/audit refs all 'synthetic'/'metadata_only'); no real client/matter/credential/secret data and no Matter-scoped data appear. The contract definition, fixture mirror, RP00 validator coverage, and README handoff are mutually consistent and bind completesSourceMicroPhase=false with next_subphase=RP00.P02.M07.S08, explicitly not completing RP00.P02.M07 / RP00.P02 / RP00 and not replacing H00/C00. HRX remains an untouched embedded boundary (no HRX-related changes in the diff). 515/515 tests pass. The RP00 validation 'failure' is composed exclusively of pending post-review artifacts (final gate command runs in command-evidence, the claude-review-result this run produces, and the construction-inspection PASS/verified flag), which the scaffold (claude-review-result.json status 'pending_single_valid_pack_level_review', adjudication.md 'intentionally pending', construction-inspection 'PENDING_CLAUDE_REVIEW_AND_FINAL_GATES') states are pending this exact review — these are adjudicable, not defects. One P3 transparency nit is noted.

boundary_checks.out_of_scope_file_changes: none (only the 8 in-scope files + cp00-035 scaffold; .DS_Store and 'Law Firm OS UI/' are manifest-ignored untracked items)

parsed_review.residual_risk: Low. No P0/P1/P2 findings; the implementation is metadata-only, fail-closed, frozen, and free of writes/real-data/boundary-drift, with 515/515 tests passing and full validator/contract/fixture/README consistency. Residual items are procedural, not code defects: (1) the post-review pack evidence must be finalized from this review — claude-review-result.json (review_completed, source claude_cli, model claude-opus-4-8, effort max, session_id/uuid/total_cost_usd, pass verdict), adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json (verdict PASS, production_ready true, control_plane_test_and_golden_case_set_primary_happy_path_verified true, P2 findings fixed_or_deferred); and (2) the final gate command runs (npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, validate, weighted:validate, goal:closeout:validate, spec:requirements:validate) must be recorded in command-evidence.json. These are exactly the artifacts the scaffold marks pending this review and are adjudicable. (3) The result validator hard-codes the synthetic metadata refs (golden_case_catalog_ref, unit_test_contract_ref, permission_decision_ref, audit_event_ref), so the precheck is intentionally usable only for the synthetic metadata-only path; this is correct fail-closed behavior for this stage and consistent with prior subphases, but the function is not a general-purpose runtime precheck and should not be treated as one in later (non-synthetic) phases.

raw_review_text:
All verifications are complete. The single grep "match" is a false positive (`re**fs.**length` contains the substring `fs.`); there are no I/O, DB, network, or audit-write primitives in the added code. HRX is entirely untouched. Here is my review.

I reviewed the full working-tree diff (8 implementation files) and the CP00-035 scaffold (5 evidence files) for RP00.P02.M07.S07. The implementation is a pure, frozen, metadata-only precheck that consumes the S06 audit-hint result and emits a synthetic success receipt; all 515 service tests pass, and the RP00 validator's only failures are the post-review evidence items the scaffold explicitly marks pending this review.

adjudication_notes: Claude produced pass_with_findings with no P0/P1/P2. CP00-035-F1 is P3 only: happy_path_receipt_fields intentionally lists required receipt fields, while the frozen receipt carries additional metadata keys matter_id, audit_hint_policy_ref, and decision_reason; validators explicitly assert the full receipt object. This is deferred to RP00.P02.M07.S08+ documentation/full-receipt-manifest consideration and does not block CP00-035 production readiness.
